title: Reviewer qualification criteria + onboarding (domain + bilingual + sensitivity)
context: Before scaling to more objects and languages, the project needs objective, documented criteria for what makes someone a qualified reviewer. Without this, reviewer recruitment (reviewers-002) cannot proceed with a measurable bar, and reviewer independence/COI rules cannot be enforced consistently. This task depends on review-001 being complete.
objective: Write a reviewer-qualification criteria and onboarding document that defines: objective criteria for a domain-aware reviewer (heritage/museum literacy; can verify factual fidelity + plain-language conformance); objective criteria for a qualified bilingual reviewer (native or near-native target language competence; heritage-translation experience); sensitivity-awareness requirements; the reviewer-independence rule (drafter may not be the sole reviewer); the COI-declaration requirement; the community/cultural-authority requirement for sensitive-heritage items; and the disagreement/conflict-resolution rule.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines objective criteria for a domain-aware reviewer: specific knowledge areas (heritage/museum literacy, ability to verify factual fidelity against sources, familiarity with plain-language principles) and how competence is evidenced

Defines objective criteria for a qualified bilingual reviewer: native or near-native target language competence, heritage-translation competence, and how competence is evidenced (e.g., translation portfolio, bilingual education, museum/library background)
States the sensitivity-awareness requirement: reviewers for sensitive-heritage items must demonstrate understanding of CARE principles and community-authority review
States the reviewer-independence rule: the human who ran the drafting agent may not be the sole sign-off reviewer for that deliverable
States the COI-declaration requirement: each reviewer declares COI (or absence of it) in each review handoff
States the disagreement/conflict-resolution rule: reconcile against sources/glossary → escalate to third reviewer/maintainer → conservative reading wins; recorded in review.yaml
resources: multilingual-museum-labels-review-001 (reviewer checklist — must be in place)
output: docs/reviewer-qualifications.md — objective criteria and onboarding guide for domain-aware and bilingual reviewers
